headline: JUNE STARTS QUIET, HOGS CRACK BELOW $100
subheadline: Funds in liquidation mode as growing season begins with little drama.
lead: Lean hogs broke through $100 for the first time since March, closing $99.50 after a 2.5% drop that looks like fund liquidation, not demand destruction. Grains barely budged with corn at $4.46 and beans holding $11.92, the kind of quiet that builds the next move rather than making one. June's opening session confirms what May's close warned: the funds are booking profits ahead of summer's data-light weeks.
the_takeaway: Quiet days price the next move, not the noise.
teaser: Hogs crash through $100 as funds liquidate into June's data void.

one_number.unit: miles from US border where New World Screwworm was found
one_number.context: The parasitic fly that eats livestock alive was detected just 31 miles south of the Texas border, the closest threat to US cattle operations since the 1960s eradication program. While not in today's prices, it's exactly the kind of binary risk cattle producers are pricing into summer risk premiums.

yesterdays_call.summary: Called energy's geopolitical premium unwinding faster than it built, with the energy complex in full retreat.
yesterdays_call.note: Crude closed above $90 but the Iran peace process kept the premium from rebuilding despite Trump's revision demands.

sections.title: Hogs Break Key $100 Floor
sections.body: Lean hogs dropped 2.5% to $99.50, the first close under $100 since March and the clearest sign that **summer liquidation is real**. No news drove it, just managed money stepping out ahead of June's lighter data flow. The break through $100 psychological support puts the next test at $97, where seasonal demand typically shows up. Pork cutout values haven't cracked, this is purely positioning.
sections.catalyst: No clean catalyst, looks like fund liquidation ahead of summer
sections.bottom_line: First sub-$100 close in three months signals fund exodus, not demand weakness.

sections.title: Grains Mark Time Into Growing Season
sections.body: Corn eased to $4.46, beans added a quarter-cent to $11.92, and wheat ticked up a penny to $6.14. **Nobody's making moves** with crop conditions still developing and no weather premium in sight. December corn at $4.75 shows the market's pricing normal yields, normal weather, normal everything. The real action starts when pollination weather shows up in July, not now.
sections.catalyst: USDA Crop Progress due Monday 3:00 PM, market positioning for update
sections.bottom_line: Range-bound consolidation while waiting for weather to matter.

sections.title: Oats Hit Hardest in Grain Complex
sections.body: Oats fell 2.6% to $3.47, the day's biggest grain loser as **thin trade amplified selling pressure**. Open interest in oats is a fraction of corn or wheat, so when the few funds positioned there decide to book profits, the moves get outsized. At 57% of the 52-week range, oats are pricing in adequate supplies without the weather premium that might develop later in summer.
sections.bottom_line: Thin trade makes small selling look big, no fundamental shift.

sections.title: Crude Holds Above $90 Despite Iran Revisions
sections.body: WTI gained $0.80 to $90.51 after Trump sent the Iran peace agreement back for revisions, but the **geopolitical premium stayed capped** rather than rebuilding. The Iran-Israel conflict that's roiled energy markets since April cooled with diplomatic progress, but the market's learned not to price full disruption risk until it sees actual supply cuts, not just diplomatic friction. Russia's jet fuel export ban through November adds some supply tightness, but that's aviation fuel, not crude.
sections.bottom_line: Iran tensions contained, crude holding $90 without rebuilding premium.

outside_the_pit.title: FTC opens antitrust probe into fertilizer pricing
outside_the_pit.body: The Federal Trade Commission confirmed it's investigating rising fertilizer costs as potential antitrust violations. Producers who locked in spring nitrogen at $600+ per ton are watching this one closely, even though investigations take years to resolve.

outside_the_pit.title: Russia bans jet fuel exports through November
outside_the_pit.body: Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian refineries have forced Moscow to halt jet fuel exports to ensure domestic supply. Won't move diesel or gasoline prices directly, but shows how the war keeps crimping global energy flows.

outside_the_pit.title: New World Screwworm detected 31 miles from Texas
outside_the_pit.body: The parasitic fly that devastated US livestock before the 1960s eradication program has been found just 31 miles south of the border. Not in cattle prices yet, but exactly the kind of binary disease risk that builds risk premiums slowly.

spread_to_watch.label: December corn / July corn carry
spread_to_watch.commentary: The 29-cent carry from July to December corn is **exactly where the seasonal says it should be**, pricing normal yields and normal harvest pressure. When this spread starts moving, it'll tell you whether the market's pricing weather risk or harvest abundance before the weather does.

basis.headline: Eastern Belt corn basis steady, western locations soft
basis.body: Corn basis east of the Mississippi **holding firm** as ethanol plants return from maintenance turnarounds. Western Belt locations staying soft with ample farmer selling and rail capacity opening up. The spread between eastern and western basis is back to seasonal normal after May's tightness.

weekly_thread.question: Will planting progress hit 95% complete by Friday?
weekly_thread.status_text: USDA Crop Progress at 3:00 PM today gives the first June planting update. Above 90% corn planted confirms the Belt finished on schedule, below 85% suggests weather delays that could matter for pollination timing.

the_more_you_know.title: Why $100 hogs matter more than the round number
the_more_you_know.body: Today's break below $100 lean hogs isn't about psychology, it's about **positioning mechanics**. Managed money holds its biggest hog long since 2023, nearly 40,000 contracts net long. When funds liquidate concentrated positions in thin contracts like hogs, the moves amplify. The $100 level matters because it's where technical stops cluster, and breaking it forces the next wave of selling. That's why today's 2.5% drop happened with no news catalyst-it's the positioning, not the pork.

watch_list.desc: USDA Crop Progress; above 90% corn planted keeps weather premium out
Lean hogs at $97 support level, breaks here target $94
Iran peace talks resume; above $92 crude rebuilds geopolitical premium
Weekly export sales; corn under 800K MT confirms slow pace

daily_quote.text: When one tugs at a single thing in nature, he finds it attached to the rest of the world.

source_summary: CME Group, USDA reports, Reuters energy desk, agricultural trade publications
